sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, cogn. to דָּבַב-

(1) to go slowly.

(2) to flow, Arab. ضَبَّ to flow, to drop, as water, صَبَّ to pour, to pour out, to pour upon. To this root I refer-

Hophal הֻצַּב (formed in the Chaldee manner), in a place which interpreters have vainly tortured, Nah 2:8, where I thus join the words הַהֵיכָל נָמֹוג וְהֻצַּב “the palace is dissolved, and made to flow down.”

Derivatives, צַב, and-
